cards.back:
1. Framework Agreement (FA): COMPANY <> MANUFACTURER — governs equipment supply, IP, revenue sharing
2. Carbon Service Agreement (CSA): COMPANY <> CLIENT — governs service delivery, fees, credits
3. Work Orders (WO): Project-specific — details scope, timeline, fees per engagement

FA is the backbone, CSA faces the client, WOs make it operational.

Full-service contract including Compliance as base layer + Carbon Credit generation.

Includes: MRV infrastructure, carbon accounting, credit issuance, and all 5 fee types. Model B (compliance) is embedded — without MRV, no credits possible. One CSA per client, one model per CSA.

Standalone carbon accounting contract without credit generation.

Covers: Scope 1/2/3 measurement, CSRD/CBAM reporting, data platform access. Contains an upgrade clause to CSA-A via supplementary agreement. Driven by EU regulatory demand (CSRD affects ~50,000 companies).

Jurisdiction-specific contract modifications attached to the base CSA.

DRC: 12 mandatory measures (sanctions, FPIC, benefit-sharing, anti-corruption, UBO monitoring, escrow, etc.)
Indonesia: 7 measures (language, PE management, equipment certification, data transfer)

Country Annexes address risks that the base contract cannot cover generically.

1. None: Client owns or provides equipment — COMPANY delivers services only
2. PaaS (DEFAULT): COMPANY provides Equipment-as-a-Service — monthly fee includes maintenance/warranty/replacement
3. Lease: Third-party DFI or MANUFACTURER as lessor — off-balance for COMPANY

PaaS is default because 10-year projects require guaranteed infrastructure with warranty + maintenance.

1. Structuring Fee — one-time, project design/feasibility/PDD
2. Initiation Fee — one-time, project setup and onboarding
3. SaaS Fee — recurring monthly, platform/software access
4. PaaS Fee — recurring monthly, equipment-as-a-service (Model A, DEFAULT)
5. Participation Fee — performance-based, % of credit revenue (Model A only)

Fees 2+5 shared with MANUFACTURER; Fees 1, 3, 4 stay with COMPANY.

One-time fee charged in both CSA-A and CSA-B.

Covers: Initial project design, feasibility assessment, PDD (Project Design Document) support, methodology selection, baseline scenario development.

Retained 100% by COMPANY — not shared with MANUFACTURER. Compensates upfront advisory work before recurring revenue begins.

Recurring monthly fee for Equipment-as-a-Service. Model A only, DEFAULT infrastructure option.

Includes: Hardware provision, maintenance, warranty coverage, replacement of defective units. COMPANY provides equipment to client; MANUFACTURER supplies to COMPANY under FA.

PaaS is default because 10-year carbon projects require guaranteed, maintained infrastructure throughout.

Performance-based fee calculated as a percentage of carbon credit revenue. Model A (Carbon Credits) only.

Key rules:
- Cash only — no in-kind payment (no credits as compensation)
- Shared with MANUFACTURER (split defined per Work Order)
- Silence in WO = MANUFACTURER gets 0%
- Tied to actual credit issuance and sale, not projections

cards.front: Why is PaaS the default infrastructure option?
cards.back:
Carbon projects run 10-30 years. Equipment needs guaranteed maintenance, warranty, and replacement throughout.

PaaS ensures:
- COMPANY controls equipment quality and uptime
- Warranty + maintenance included in monthly fee
- Replacement at COMPANY's/MANUFACTURER's cost
- No client capital expenditure upfront
- SLA-backed uptime guarantees (95% quarterly target)

cards.front: What is the revenue sharing principle between COMPANY and MANUFACTURER?
cards.back:
No default split exists. Each fee has a 'Compensation' field in the Work Order — silence means MANUFACTURER gets 0%.

Shared fees: Initiation Fee + Participation Fee
COMPANY-only fees: Structuring, SaaS, PaaS

The exact split is negotiated per project and documented in the Work Order. [Reserved] placeholder protects minimum margins.

[Reserved] in all documents — ensures COMPANY retains a minimum margin regardless of how fees are structured or shared with MANUFACTURER.

Appears in: contracts, briefings, management summaries. Prevents scenarios where aggressive revenue sharing with MANUFACTURER erodes COMPANY's viability. Exact mechanism is an open management decision.

cards.front: Which fees are shared with MANUFACTURER?
cards.back:
Shared: Initiation Fee + Participation Fee (split per Work Order)
COMPANY retains 100%: Structuring Fee, SaaS Fee, PaaS Fee

Rationale: MANUFACTURER contributes to project setup (Initiation) and benefits from credit success (Participation). Platform, advisory, and equipment management are COMPANY's value-add.

cards.front: What is CPI adjustment for fees?
cards.back:
Annual fee adjustment mechanism to protect against inflation over 10-30 year project lifetimes.

Open decision: Which index to use?
- Swiss LIK (Landesindex der Konsumentenpreise) — COMPANY's home jurisdiction
- US CPI — MANUFACTURER's jurisdiction and USD-denominated market

Must be defined in the CSA to avoid disputes over long contract terms.

cards.front: What is the 'Original Purchaser' warranty problem?
cards.back:
MANUFACTURER's standard warranty covers only COMPANY (the direct purchaser), not the end-client who actually uses the equipment.

In a 3-party setup (MANUFACTURER > COMPANY > CLIENT), the client has no direct warranty claim against MANUFACTURER. This gap must be closed contractually via pass-through warranty provisions.

cards.front: What is the warranty duration problem?
cards.back:
Standard: 24 months from SHIPMENT (not installation).

Problem: In DRC, transport takes 4-8 weeks due to poor infrastructure (19% electricity, 'sparsest roads in the world'). Effective warranty on-site = only 20-22 months.

Solution: FA Art. 12.4 pass-through warranty extends coverage to 24 months from INSTALLATION date.

cards.front: What is the Pass-Through Warranty solution?
cards.back:
FA Art. 12.4: MANUFACTURER acknowledges client warranty claims and extends coverage to 24 months from INSTALLATION (not shipment).

This closes two gaps:
1. Warranty reaches the actual end-user (client)
2. Coverage starts when equipment is operational, not when it leaves the factory

Critical for remote locations where transport significantly reduces effective warranty.

cards.front: What is the lease advantage for warranty?
cards.back:
Lease covers the entire lease term — not just 24 months:
- Replacement at MANUFACTURER's cost
- No client return shipping responsibility
- Maintenance included throughout

Limitation: MANUFACTURER cannot be direct lessor under UCC Finance Lease (requires 3-party structure: Supplier > Lessor > Lessee). COMPANY or third-party DFI must serve as lessor.

cards.front: Why can't MANUFACTURER be the direct lessor?
cards.back:
UCC Finance Lease requires a 3-party structure: Supplier > Lessor > Lessee. The manufacturer cannot simultaneously be supplier AND lessor for off-balance-sheet treatment.

Options:
- COMPANY as lessor (PaaS model, on COMPANY's balance sheet)
- Third-party DFI as lessor (true off-balance for both)
- MANUFACTURER leasing arm (separate legal entity)

COMPANY provides equipment to the client under a monthly service fee. Includes:
- Hardware provision (sourced from MANUFACTURER)
- Preventive and corrective maintenance
- Warranty coverage for the service period
- Replacement of defective/failed units
- SLA-backed uptime guarantee (95% quarterly)

Equipment has built-in redundancy — single unit failure does not break the measurement chain.
